title: Verifier Plugin ABI v1
description: Application Binary Interface for verifier plugins. Defines input/output schema, lifecycle, sandbox requirements, capability flags, and error handling.

required_fields.description: Unique identifier for the plugin. Format: <check_type>.<variant>. Example: unit.jest, integration.http, freshness.web.
Which check type this plugin implements.
Declares what resources the plugin needs.

capability_flags.description: Flags a plugin declares to indicate required resources. The engine grants or denies based on sandbox policy.

requires_fs_read.description: Plugin needs to read files from the workspace.

requires_fs_write.description: Plugin needs to write temporary files (test output, screenshots).

requires_network.description: Plugin needs network access (API checks, freshness lookups).

requires_web.description: Plugin needs web browser access (visual checks, e2e tests).

requires_process_exec.description: Plugin needs to execute subprocesses (test runners, build tools).

capability_flags.enforcement: Capabilities not declared but attempted at runtime are denied by the sandbox.

input_schema.description: What the engine passes to a plugin on invocation.

required.description: Unique ID for this plugin invocation. Used for correlation.
Workflow correlation ID. Echoed into output.
Human-readable criterion description from the ExecutionPlan.
Resolved evidence references from the evidence registry. Each contains evidence_id, path, type, and hash.
The check type to execute (unit, integration, etc.).
Current workflow class, for context-dependent checks.

optional.description: Plugin-specific configuration (test runner args, thresholds, etc.).
Time-sensitive claims to verify (freshness check type only).

output_schema.description: What the plugin returns to the engine after execution.

required.description: Plugin verdict. Note: 'error' indicates plugin failure, mapped to 'blocked' by engine.
List of evidence_ids the plugin actually consumed.

optional.description: Plugin-specific detail (test counts, failure messages, screenshot paths, etc.).
New evidence artifacts produced by the plugin (screenshots, test reports). Registered into the evidence registry.

phases.description: Engine discovers and loads the plugin. Validates plugin_id, check_type, version, and capability_flags.
phases.on_failure: Plugin marked unavailable. Criteria requiring this plugin get status: blocked.

phases.description: Engine passes plugin_config if provided. Plugin validates its own configuration.
phases.on_failure: Plugin returns error status with rationale.

phases.description: Engine invokes plugin with input_schema. Plugin runs verification and returns output_schema.
Engine releases plugin resources. Temporary files are cleaned up. Sandbox is torn down.
phases.on_failure: Logged but does not affect verdict.

resource_policy.enforcement: Sandbox enforces limits. Exceeding any limit terminates the plugin with error status.

sandbox_requirements.description: Plugins execute in an isolated sandbox to prevent interference with the workflow or other plugins.
rules: Plugin cannot access workflow state, routing, or plan content.
Plugin can only access evidence referenced in evidence_refs.
Plugin file writes are confined to a temporary directory.
Plugin network access is gated by capability_flags.requires_network.
Plugin subprocess execution is gated by capability_flags.requires_process_exec.

plugin_errors.engine_response: Set criteria status to blocked. Log timeout event.
Set criteria status to blocked. Log resource violation.
Set criteria status to blocked. Log crash with stack trace if available.
Set criteria status to blocked. Log schema validation failure.
Set criteria status to blocked. Log capability violation.

error_handling.invariant: No plugin error can produce a pass or warn status. All errors are fail-closed to blocked.

abi_invariants.rule: Plugin output must echo invocation_id and correlation_id from input.
Plugin cannot modify evidence in the registry. It can only read referenced evidence and produce new evidence.
Plugin status 'error' is always mapped to 'blocked' by the engine. Plugins cannot produce 'blocked' directly.
Capability flags are declared at load time and cannot be changed during execution.
Each plugin invocation is stateless. No state persists between invocations.
